sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current M squares:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Compare with previous frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    prev_nodes = prev_seg['nodes']
    print("\nPrevious M squares:")
    for n in prev_nodes:
        if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
            print(f"  Node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so Node 9 moved from [33-34, 37-38] to [32-33, 36-37]. That's up-left by 1. So clicking at [33, 37] (which is the top-left corner of Node 9) moved it up-left by 1.
But earlier, clicking at [34, 38] (which is the bottom-right corner of Node 9) didn't move it. And clicking at [34, 38] when Node 9 was at [34-35, 38-39] did move it up-left by 1.
So it seems like the click position relative to the magenta square matters. Let me think about this...
When Node 9 was at [34-35, 38-39], clicking at [34, 38] (top-left corner) moved it up-left by 1.
When Node 9 was at [33-34, 37-38], clicking at [34, 38] (bottom-right corner) didn't move it.
When Node 9 was at [33-34, 37-38], clicking at [33, 37] (top-left corner) moved it up-left by 1.
So it seems like clicking on the top-left corner of a magenta square moves it up-left by 1. Clicking on other corners doesn't do anything (or does something else).
Wait, but earlier I clicked at [35, 39] when Node 10 was at [35-36, 39-40], and that caused a swap with Node 9. [35, 39] is the top-left corner of Node 10. So clicking on the top-left corner of Node 10 caused a swap, not just moving up-left by 1.
Hmm, so the top-left corner click behavior is inconsistent. Let me think about this more carefully...
Actually, maybe the behavior depends on whether there's another magenta square nearby. When I clicked on the top-left corner of Node 10, Node 9 was nearby (to the left), and they swapped. When I clicked on the top-left corner of Node 9, there was no magenta square nearby in the top-left direction, so it just moved up-left by 1.
But wait, Node 10 was at [35-36, 16-17] after the swap, and Node 9 was at [34-35, 38-39]. So Node 10 is to the left of Node 9. When I clicked on the top-left corner of Node 9, Node 10 is to the left (but not in the top-left direction). So maybe the swap only happens if there's a magenta square in the exact top-left direction.
Actually, I'm spending too much time trying to understand the exact mechanic. Let me focus on the goal.
Node 9 is at [32-33, 36-37], and the blue shape is at [23-31, 29-37]. Node 9's column range [36-37] overlaps with the blue shape's column range [29-37] at columns 36-37. And Node 9's row range [32-33] is below the blue shape's row range [23-31]. So Node 9 needs to move up by about 1-2 rows to reach the blue shape.
Let me continue clicking on Node 9 to move it into the blue shape.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Node 9 is at [32-33, 36-37], blue shape is at [23-31, 29-37]
# Node 9 needs to move up by ~1-2 to reach the blue shape
# Let me click on Node 9's top-left corner at [32, 36]
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 36}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
